title: Client-server architecture with WebSocket
description: Refactor from monolithic to client-server per design/technical.md. Server hosts GameLoop, clients connect via WebSocket. Server validates all actions, streams events to all clients.
close_reason: Client-server architecture fully implemented: protocol.py, serialize.py, server (GameServer + WebSocketPlayerInput), client bridge (ClientBridge), TUI integration (local/networked modes), main.py CLI modes (--local, --server, --client), websockets dependency added

title: Implement central game loop and PlayerInput interface
description: Build GameLoop orchestrator that drives TurnEngine + EventPipeline + PlayerInput through the full game lifecycle. Includes text-based PlayerInput implementation and main.py entry point.
close_reason: Implemented GameLoop, PlayerInput ABC, TextPlayerInput, and main.py. TurnEngine refactored to route all events through EventPipeline.

title: Implement dice display widget
description: 7x7 or 5x5 ASCII dice display in top-left corner. Shows rolling animation (flashing random numbers) then settles on actual roll. Updates to show remaining moves during movement phase. Part of TUI P0 epic.

title: Implement info command system
description: Implement 'info' command: info player (full stats), info square (square definition), info game (victory condition, board settings). This is how the game is playable before full UI. Part of TUI P0 epic.

title: Implement command input and validation
description: Implement the command prompt that shows available options (e.g. [R]oll, [S]ell Stock, [V]iew Board) and accepts single-key input. Client-side validation before sending to server. Part of TUI P0 epic.

title: Implement scrollable game log
description: Implement the log panel that shows game events in chronological order. Must be scrollable back to start of game. Shows [LOG] prefixed messages, [ERROR] for invalid inputs. Auto-scrolls to newest. Part of TUI P0 epic.

title: Set up Textual app skeleton and screen layout
description: Create the base Textual app with the main screen layout: game area (top), log/command area (bottom 8-10 rows). Set up the app lifecycle and basic key handling. Part of TUI P0 epic.

title: Implement forced asset liquidation on negative cash
description: At end of turn, if ready cash < 0: force player to sell stock and/or properties until cash >= 0. Properties sold for 75% value to bank, then auctioned to other players at 100% starting bid. If no bids, property becomes unowned. Part of Bankruptcy epic.

title: Implement victory condition and game end
description: When player passes bank with net worth >= target: they win and claim 1st place. Remaining players ranked by net worth. Game ends. Transition to victory/recap screen. Part of Victory epic.

title: Implement bankruptcy detection and resolution
description: If player net worth < 0 at any point: immediate bankruptcy. Rent owed is still fully paid. Player's stock is sold at market price. Properties are auctioned (75% to player, then auction at 100% value starting bid). Player removed from game. Check max_bankruptcies setting. Part of Bankruptcy epic.

title: Implement Commission, Closed, and Discount/PriceHike statuses
description: Commission X: player earns X% of all rent transactions between any players. Closed: shop pays 0 rent when landed on. Discount/PriceHike X: shop rent temporarily +/- X%. Part of Status Effects epic.

title: Implement status effect system and duration tracking
description: Create base StatusEffect class with type, modifier/level X, remaining turns. Auto-decrement at end of turn cycle. Remove when expired. Support player statuses and shop statuses. Part of Status Effects epic.

title: Implement Take a Break, Boon, and Roll On square behaviors
description: Take a Break: land = all player's shops gain Closed status for 1 turn cycle. Boon: land = player gains Commission 20% status. Roll On: land = player rolls dice again and continues moving. Part of P0 Square Types epic.

title: Implement Suit and Venture square behaviors
description: Suit square: pass = collect suit if not owned. Land = draw venture card. Venture square: land = draw venture card. No pass event. Part of P0 Square Types epic.

title: Implement Shop square behavior
description: Shop square: land on unowned = buy option. Land on own = invest option (any owned shop). Land on opponent = pay rent. No pass event. Part of P0 Square Types epic.

title: Implement Bank square behavior
description: Bank square: pass event = buy stock opportunity + check promotion (if all suits) + check victory (if target net worth reached). Land event = choose direction. Promotion happens before stock buying. Part of P0 Square Types epic.

title: Implement promotion at bank
description: When player passes bank with all 4 suits: trigger promotion. Calculate bonus: base_salary + salary_increment*(level-1) + shop_value_multiplier*total_shop_value + comeback_bonus. Comeback = comeback_multiplier * (1st_place_networth - player_networth) if level >= 4 and not in 1st. Reset suits. Increment level. Part of Promotion System epic.

title: Implement stock price calculation and updates
description: Stock price = value_component + fluctuation_component. Value component = 4% of total district property value (rounded nearest int). Fluctuation changes: buy/sell >= 10 shares changes by (total_price // 16 + 1). Fluctuation updates apply at end of turn. Value updates apply immediately on investment. Part of Stock Market epic.

title: Implement suit collection on suit squares
description: When player passes a suit square, grant them that suit if they don't have it. Track suits in player state dict. Change of Suit squares grant current suit then rotate (P1 but collection logic is P0). Part of Promotion System epic.

title: Implement stock selling mechanics
description: Players can sell stock: pre-roll (any amount, any districts), forced (when cash negative, sell until >= 0), or event-triggered. Add cash, remove stock from player. Part of Stock Market epic.

title: Implement stock buying mechanics
description: Players can buy stock at bank (pass), stockbroker (land), or event triggers. Can only buy one district per opportunity. Can only buy what current ready cash covers. Deduct cash, add stock to player. Part of Stock Market epic.

title: Implement rent and max capital lookup tables
description: Create the LUT data structures for rent multiplier and max capital multiplier, keyed by (num_shops_owned_in_district, num_shops_in_district). These are used in rent and max cap formulas. Part of Property System epic.

title: Create a test board for development
description: Design and implement a small but complete test board (15-25 squares) with: bank, shops in 2-3 districts, suit squares (all 4), venture squares, at least one intersection, and basic loop path. Export as board file. Part of Board System epic.

title: Implement shop investment
description: When player lands on own shop: may invest in any owned shop up to max capital (MaxCap = LUT * BaseVal - CurrentVal). Investment increases current value by amount invested. Part of Property System epic.

title: Implement rent payment system
description: When player lands on opponent's shop: calculate current rent using LUT formula (Rent = LUT * BaseRent * (2*CurrentVal - BaseVal) / BaseVal), deduct from landing player, add to owner. Part of Property System epic.

title: Implement shop buying and ownership
description: When player lands on unowned shop and can afford it: option to buy for base value, transfer ownership. Track district ownership counts. Part of Property System epic.

title: Implement pathfinding and movement logic on board graph
description: Given a player's current position and from_square, determine valid next squares using waypoints. Support intersection choices (multiple to_ids). Handle special movement: doorways (teleport mid-roll, no move cost), backstreets (warp). Part of Board System epic.

title: Implement end-of-turn processing
description: At end of turn: check if player's ready cash is negative and force asset sales, apply pending stock price fluctuation changes, decrement status effect durations and remove expired ones, advance to next player. Part of Turn & Movement epic.

title: Implement player movement with intersection choices
description: Move player step-by-step along waypoints, triggering pass events at each square. When multiple to_ids exist, pause for player choice. Track remaining moves. Support undo (go back to previous intersection). Part of Turn & Movement epic.

title: Define board file format and implement board loader
description: Design a JSON/YAML file format for board definitions. Implement a loader that reads the file and constructs a BoardState with all squares, waypoints, districts, and settings. Part of Board System epic.

title: Implement dice rolling and turn state machine
description: Implement dice roll (1 to max_dice_roll), turn phases (pre-roll actions, roll, movement, land, end-of-turn). Track whose turn it is and cycle through active players. Part of Turn & Movement epic.

title: Implement event execution pipeline
description: Create the event queue/pipeline that processes events sequentially, supports chaining (events that trigger other events), and maintains event history for the game log. Part of Event System epic.

title: Implement GameEvent base class with serialization
description: Rewrite event.py from starter_code. Abstract base with execute(state), to_dict(), from_dict(), get_result(). Serialization excludes private fields. Uses registry for deserialization. Part of Event System epic.

title: Implement event registry with @register_event decorator
description: Rewrite register.py from starter_code. Registry maps class name strings to event classes. @register_event decorator auto-registers. get_class() for deserialization lookup. Part of Event System epic.

title: Implement GameState dataclass
description: Rewrite gamestate.py from starter_code. GameState with current_player, board_state, stock_state, player_states list, cameo_state list, events. Part of Core Data Models epic.

title: Implement StockState and StockPrice dataclasses
description: Rewrite stockstate.py from starter_code. StockState with list of StockPrice indexed by district. StockPrice with district_id, value_component, fluctuation_component, and current_price property. Part of Core Data Models epic.

title: Implement PlayerState dataclass
description: Rewrite playerstate.py from starter_code. PlayerState with player_id, position, from_square, ready_cash, level, suits dict, owned_properties list, owned_stock dict, statuses list. Part of Core Data Models epic.

title: Implement BoardState, SquareInfo, Waypoint, PromotionInfo dataclasses
description: Rewrite boardstate.py from starter_code. BoardState with max_dice_roll, promotion_info, target_networth, max_bankruptcies, squares list. SquareInfo with id, position, waypoints, type, statuses, events, and all square-specific fields. Waypoint with from_id and to_ids. Part of Core Data Models epic.

title: Implement Suit enum and SquareType enum
description: Rewrite suit.py and square type enum from starter_code into proper library. Suit enum with SPADE, HEART, DIAMOND, CLUB, WILD and next() method. SquareType enum with all square types. Part of Core Data Models epic.

title: Set up virtual environment and install dependencies
description: Create venv, install textual, ruff, mypy/pyright. Verify everything works. Add .gitignore entries for venv, __pycache__, etc.

title: Set up Python project with pyproject.toml and package structure
description: Create pyproject.toml with project metadata, set up src/ package layout (road_to_riches/), configure ruff for linting/formatting, add type checking config. Create requirements.txt or use pyproject.toml deps for textual and dev tools.

title: [Epic] Terminal UI Client (P0)
description: Build the P0 TUI using Python Textual library. Includes: command line + scrollable log (bottom 8-10 rows), player input with validation, info command system (info player/square/game), dice display (7x7 or 5x5 with animation). Terminal minimum 140x35. This is the minimum viable client.

title: [Epic] Status Effects (P0)
description: Implement P0 status effect system: Commission X (earn X% of all rent transactions), Closed (shop pays 0 rent), Discount/Price Hike X (shop rent +/- X%). Statuses have duration in turns and auto-expire.

title: [Epic] P0 Square Types
description: Implement all P0 square type behaviors: Bank (stock buying, promotion, victory), Shop (buy/rent/invest), Suit (collect suit + venture card), Venture (draw venture card), Take a Break (close shops), Boon (20% commission), Roll On (roll again). Each square has pass and land events.

title: [Epic] Bankruptcy & Victory
description: Implement bankruptcy (net worth < 0 = immediate elimination, full rent still paid, stock sold, properties auctioned), victory condition (reach target net worth + return to bank), game end flow, and configurable max bankruptcies.

title: [Epic] Promotion System
description: Implement suit collection (passing suit squares), promotion at bank when all 4 suits collected, promotion bonus calculation (base salary + level bonus + shop bonus + comeback bonus), level tracking. Suits reset after promotion.

title: [Epic] Stock Market System (P0)
description: Implement P0 stock mechanics: buying stock (at bank/stockbroker/events), selling stock (pre-roll/forced), stock price calculation (value_component + fluctuation_component), price changes on buy/sell >= 10 shares, price updates on property investment. Property component = 4% of total district value.

title: [Epic] Property System (P0)
description: Implement P0 property mechanics: buying unowned shops, paying rent on opponent shops, investing in own shops, district ownership tracking. Includes rent/max-capital LUT formulas: Rent = LUT * BaseRent * (2*CurrentVal - BaseVal) / BaseVal, MaxCap = LUT * BaseVal - CurrentVal.

title: [Epic] Board System
description: Implement board loading from file format, square graph with waypoint-based movement/pathfinding, board state management, and at least one test board. Boards have branching paths and dynamic layouts.

title: [Epic] Turn & Movement Engine
description: Implement the core turn loop: pre-roll actions, dice rolling, movement along waypoints with intersection choices, pass/land event triggering, and end-of-turn processing (negative cash resolution, stock price updates).

title: [Epic] Event System Framework
description: Implement the event system: GameEvent base class, event registry with @register_event decorator, serialization/deserialization, and event execution pipeline. This is the backbone for all game logic.

title: [Epic] Core Data Models
description: Implement all core dataclasses: GameState, PlayerState, BoardState, StockState, Suit enum, SquareType enum, SquareInfo, Waypoint, PromotionInfo. Rewrite from starter_code into proper library with type hints and validation.

title: [Epic] Project Setup & Infrastructure
description: Set up Python project structure, virtual environment, dependencies (textual, ruff), pyproject.toml, and base package layout. This is the foundation everything else depends on.

title: Basic AI client (greedy suit collector)
description: A standalone AI client process that connects via WebSocket and plays automatically. Strategy: (1) Pathfinding: BFS shortest path to collect all 4 static suit squares then reach bank for promotion. Recompute each turn. Ignore change-of-suit squares. (2) Shop buying: always buy empty shops when affordable. (3) Stock buying: at bank/stockbroker, buy max stock in district with highest total max_capital across all players' shops, with slight weight toward own shops. (4) Investment: prioritize shops in districts where AI owns stock. (5) All other decisions: sensible defaults (always roll, confirm stop on target square, skip forced buyout, etc). (6) Configurable response delay (default ~0.5s) so human can follow. Should be a simple script in src/road_to_riches/ai/ that the server spawns.

title: Server auto-spawns AI client subprocesses
description: When launching a game with AI players, the server should spawn AI client subprocesses that connect back via WebSocket. CLI: specify human count and AI count (e.g. --humans 1 --ai 3, default 1 human 3 AI). Server assigns player IDs: humans first, then AIs. Server spawns one subprocess per AI player, passing the WebSocket URL and assigned player_id. AI subprocess connects, identifies itself, and plays using the standard protocol. Server waits for all human clients to connect before starting.

title: Per-player input routing in server
description: Server currently broadcasts all input requests to all clients and accepts response from any. Need to: (1) assign each WebSocket client a player_id on connect, (2) attach player_id to every input_request message, (3) only accept input_response from the client matching that player_id, (4) all clients still receive all events (state_sync, log, dice) for state tracking. This is a prereq for AI players since AI and human clients must only respond to their own prompts.

title: Binary board file converter from original game
description: Build a converter that reads the original Fortune Street (Itadaki Street) serialized binary board files and outputs board JSON files compatible with our system. Requires reverse-engineering the binary format: square types, positions, waypoints, districts, shop values, etc.
notes: Converter working. Parses FRB binary format (I4DT/I4PL headers, 32-byte square records). Handles all known square types, bidirectional waypoints, backstreet/doorway destinations, position scaling (original 64 units = our 4 units). Tested on basic.frb and bobomb.frb. Districts counted from actual property data.

title: WASD directional movement for path selection
description: Replace typed square-ID input with WASD key-press navigation during movement. Calculate direction from current square to each valid next square using board positions, map to WASD keys. No enter required — keypress submits immediately. Confirm-stop dialog also uses keypress (S/U). Other prompts keep normal Input+Enter flow.
close_reason: WASD directional movement implemented with direction.py module, keypress mode in TUI, 12 tests

title: Implement board editor with export to board file format
description: Build the board editor that outputs valid board files. Must support all square types, waypoint configuration, district assignment, and board settings (dice range, target net worth, etc). Part of Board Editor epic.

title: Implement Fixed Price X shop status
description: Fixed Price X: shop rent is temporarily fixed to X gold, independent of shop value/formula. Duration in turns. Part of Status Effects P1 epic.

title: Design board editor UI and workflow
description: Plan the board editor application: how users place squares on a grid, set types, configure waypoints/connections, assign districts, set board properties. Could be TUI or simple web app. Part of Board Editor epic.

title: Implement stock price change camera animation
description: When stock price changes, camera should pan to the district (average position of properties) and display UI showing the change and profit/loss for each player. Part of Stock Market P1 epic.

title: Implement dividend system
description: When rent is paid in a district with stockholders: 20% of rent value is split as dividends among stockholders weighted by shares owned. Dividends paid by bank (new money enters economy). Part of Stock Market P1 epic.
close_reason: Stockbroker: offers BUY_STOCK + SELL_STOCK on land, with logging. Dividends: 20% of rent split proportionally among district stockholders (paid by bank), integrated into PayRentEvent, with game loop logging.

title: Implement Stockbroker square
description: Stockbroker: land = player may buy stock (same as bank stock buying, but triggered by landing). Part of P1 Squares epic.
close_reason: Stockbroker: offers BUY_STOCK + SELL_STOCK on land, with logging. Dividends: 20% of rent split proportionally among district stockholders (paid by bank), integrated into PayRentEvent, with game loop logging.

title: Implement Backstreet, Doorway, Cannon, Switch squares
description: Backstreet: land = warp to destination, no pass/land at destination. Doorway: pass = teleport to paired doorway, continue remaining roll (no move cost). Cannon: land = select player, jump to their location. Switch: land = change board layout state. Part of P1 Squares epic.

title: Implement Change of Suit, Suit Yourself, and Boom squares
description: Change of Suit: pass = grant current suit, rotate to next. Land = venture card. Suit Yourself: land = grant wild card. Boom: land = 50% commission status. Part of P1 Squares epic.

title: Implement property renovation
description: Owner can renovate vacant plot property into different type. Receives 75% of current value back, pays 100% of new type price. E.g. 200->200 costs 50G. Part of Vacant Plots epic.

title: Implement Tax Office property type
description: Tax Office: rent based on landing player's net worth. Owner land: receive 4% of own net worth. Other player land: pay 4% of their net worth to owner. Part of Vacant Plots epic.

title: Implement Checkpoint property type
description: Checkpoint: toll paid on pass AND land. Initial toll 10. Owner pass/land: raise toll by 10. Owner land also allows investing in any owned shop. Other player pass/land: pay toll, raise by 10. Part of Vacant Plots epic.

title: Implement forced buyout mechanic
description: After paying rent, landing player may force-buy the shop for 5x value. Owner receives 3x value, 2x goes to bank. Only available if player can afford 5x. Part of Shop Exchanges epic.

title: Implement vacant plot purchase and development choice
description: When player lands on vacant plot: buy it and immediately choose development type (Checkpoint or Tax Office). Create the chosen property type. Part of Vacant Plots epic.

title: Implement shop trade system
description: Player A selects 1-2 of their shops and 1-2 of player B's shops, optionally adding/requesting gold. B can accept, counter-offer, or reject. Part of Shop Exchanges epic.

title: Implement shop buy/sell negotiation
description: Player A can offer to buy one of player B's shops at a proposed price. B can accept, counter-offer, or reject. Reverse for selling. Transfer property and gold on acceptance. Part of Shop Exchanges epic.

title: Implement shop auction system
description: Player puts own shop up for auction. Other players bid. Highest bidder wins and pays bid to seller. If no bids, seller gets base value and shop becomes unowned. Part of Shop Exchanges epic.

title: Implement starter set of venture cards
description: Implement 15-20 venture cards covering key categories: movement (choose direction, roll again, warp), economic (shop growth %, stock price changes, receive/pay gold), forced actions (sell a shop, forced to buy stock). Each card is a registered event. Part of Venture Card epic.

title: Implement venture card framework and deck system
description: Create VentureCard base class that wraps events. Implement venture deck per board: shuffle, draw, reshuffle when empty. Cards are data-driven and defined in board files. Part of Venture Card epic.

title: Implement board browsing mode
description: When player presses V to view board: hide all UI except game view. WASD to pan view. Center square shows detailed info overlay in top-right. Press V or Esc to return to normal view. Part of TUI P0.5 epic.

title: Implement stock market overlay view
description: Full-screen overlay table for stock buying/selling/viewing. Players as columns, districts as rows with stock prices. Cells show number of stocks owned. Used whenever stock interaction happens. Part of TUI P0.5 epic.

title: Implement player info overlay panel
description: Bottom-right overlay (above log) showing all players' info: name, net worth, ready cash, suits collected, current status, level. Each player's box bordered in their color. Compact 2-line layout per player. Part of TUI P0.5 epic.

title: Implement board game view with ASCII grid squares
description: Render board as 8x4 (actually 8-wide x 4-tall per square, since chars are 2x tall as wide) grid with box-drawing borders. Each square shows type-specific content (shop value/rent, suit name, etc). District-colored borders for shops, player-colored text. Square ID in bottom-right, player IDs in bottom-left. Part of TUI P0.5 epic.

title: [Epic] Terminal UI Client (P0.5)
description: Build the P0.5 TUI features: Game View (8x4 grid squares with box-drawing borders, district colors, player colors, square IDs), Player Info View (bottom-right overlay showing net worth/cash/suits/status/level for all players), Stock Market View (overlay table for buy/sell/view stocks). Minimum 140x35 terminal.

title: [Epic] Venture Card System
description: Implement the venture card framework and a starter set of venture cards. Cards should be data-driven and easy to add. Examples: choose direction, roll again, shop growth %, stock price changes, forced sells, warps, spawning cameo characters. Each board defines its own venture deck.

title: [Epic] Board Editor (P1)
description: Create a separate board editor application for designing board layout files. Should allow placing squares, setting types, configuring waypoints/connections, assigning districts, and exporting to the game's board file format.

title: [Epic] Status Effects (P1)
description: Implement P1 status effects: Fixed Price X (shop rent temporarily fixed to X gold regardless of value). Extend the status system to support this new type alongside existing P0 statuses.

title: [Epic] Stock Market P1 Features
description: Implement P1 stock features: Dividends (20% of rent transactions split by stock weight, paid by bank), stock info viewing UI (players x districts table), stock price change camera animation. Players can view stock holdings at any input time during their turn.

title: [Epic] P1 Square Types
description: Implement remaining P1 square types: Change of Suit (rotating suit), Suit Yourself (grants wild card), Boom (50% commission), Backstreet (warp to destination), Doorway (teleport mid-roll, doesn't consume moves), Cannon (jump to another player), Switch (change board layout), Stockbroker (buy stock).

title: [Epic] Vacant Plots (P1)
description: Implement vacant plot system: purchase vacant plot and choose development type (Checkpoint or Tax Office). Checkpoint: toll paid on pass, toll increases by 10 each interaction. Tax Office: rent = 4% of landing player's net worth. Renovation: pay to switch type (75% refund + 100% new cost).

title: [Epic] Shop Exchanges & Forced Buyouts (P1)
description: Implement P1 property trading: Buy (offer to buy opponent's shop), Sell (offer your shop to opponent), Auction (auction your shop to all players), Trade (swap 1-2 shops with optional gold). Also forced buyouts: after paying rent, option to buy shop for 5x value (owner gets 3x, 2x to bank).

title: Fix browse mode (E) skipping some squares
description: Browse mode in the TUI is skipping some squares when navigating with WASD. Need to ask user for specifics on which squares are being skipped and reproduce the issue. Likely related to get_board_grid() or the navigation logic in _handle_browse_key().

title: Dev command: undo previous turn
description: Add a dev/debug command that undoes the previous player's entire turn, restoring the game state to the start of that turn. Requires snapshotting full GameState (board, players, stock, pipeline history) at turn start. The existing undo system only handles movement steps within a turn via MoveSnapshot — this needs a deeper full-state snapshot/restore mechanism.

title: Dice widget: count down remaining moves during movement
description: The dice remaining counter should count down as the player moves, not stay fixed at the roll value. The number shown below the dice is the remaining moves counter.

title: TUI: WASD selection bar for all player input
description: Replace the current text-input and keypress modes with a unified selection bar UI. All player choices should appear as a horizontal list of options that the player navigates with W/D (or A/D) and selects with Space. Backspace acts as a general 'no/cancel/skip'. This replaces: typed Y/N prompts, typed numeric IDs, typed multi-field strings, and the existing keypress mode. Covers all 18 InputRequestType cases.
close_reason: Implemented WASD selection bar for all TUI input types except CHOOSE_PATH (keeps directional WASD) and TRADE/COUNTER_PRICE (keep text input). Selection mode uses A/W to move left, D/S to move right, Space to confirm, Backspace to cancel. Two-phase flows (stock buy/sell, invest, auction bid, shop buy/sell) use selection then text input for quantity/price.

title: Dice widget: show empty face at 0 remaining moves
description: When remaining moves reaches 0, the dice should display an empty face (no pips) rather than showing the rolled value.

title: Dice widget: resize to 9x5 for square aspect
description: Current dice is 5x5 characters which looks tall/narrow. Resize to 9x5 (inner 7x3) so it appears square in monospace terminal layout.
